title: Object 2
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 2: “Level Keys”}} {{VoidedTheme|mono}} '''Object 2''', commonly referred to as '''level keys''', are extremely rare objects found across the Backrooms which access the entrances of levels. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=A Stash of Level Keys.jpg|caption=A stash of '''level keys'''}} For every numbered and anomalous level in the Backrooms[[:Category:Locations|Locations]] are the only levels to not have a '''level key'''., there is a '''level key''', which varies in appearance depending on its respective level. Said level is identified by a runic symbol engraved into a small rectangular label on the handle of the key, or other similar area in event of a uniquely-shaped key. The symbol on each key for each level is unique, corresponding with the symbols above their respective level doors within [[The Hub]]. Due to each '''level key''' bearing a unique design, not all of them are represented as standard keys like those found in [[The Frontrooms]]. In certain instances, some may manifest in more abstract forms, unpredictable in function. They may even present themselves as other objects or entities. ==Properties== A '''level key'''’s primary function is to reveal and access entrances to its corresponding level. Aside from the officially documented entrances/exits of levels, there are hidden ones which cannot normally be identified nor located. By uniting the key with such hidden entrances, they will thus be revealed and unlocked. However, they will commonly then disappear after being entered, needing to be revealed and unlocked by its key once again. All '''level keys''' resonate with their respective levels at a degree of intensity relative to their proximity to an entry point. This is foundational to the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]], which designates levels with numbers based on the measurement of their respective key’s resonance in radio frequency. When held whilst asleep, '''level keys''' can occasionally induce dreams about their respective level. These dreams typically consist of wandering the same area of the level repeatedly, which is capable of inducing madness upon wanderers who are afflicted with narcolepsy. The inner workings of '''level keys''' are yet unknown, as active research by the [[M.E.G.]] remains underway. Attempted analysis of the material from which '''level keys''' are made has yielded inconclusive results. ==Location== '''Level keys''' are extremely difficult to locate, meaning that the chances of a wanderer coming across one in their lifetime are very slim at best. Lost or discarded '''level keys''' may be found in the crevices of various levels throughout the Backrooms, more commonly occurring in levels that are least accessed from [[The Hub]]. Searching for '''level keys''' is considered a fruitless endeavor, as they are more likely found by pure chance. Given their rarity and value, they are often bartered for high prices amongst [[groups]]. If one wishes to search for '''level keys''', there are three methods commonly used. First, one can shine a flashlight into dark areas of a level in hopes of triggering the flashbang-like reflection of the '''level key''''s label. This method has the least chance of success, and as one is wandering through dark areas of levels, the chances of [[Entity 3|Smiler]] attack is high. Second, trading with other wanderers for '''level keys''' is the easiest and most reliable method of finding a '''level key'''. This method may come at steep prices of other resources, such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. Finally, a radio on Scan mode can be used to hop frequencies to detect the emissions of '''level keys'''. This is the most practical method of searching and comes at the least cost. It can be used on all levels, as long as the properties of the level do not block radio transmission. However, it takes the most time and has a low chance of success. ==Origin== It is unknown what mechanism creates '''level keys''', nor how they are found across the Backrooms. They exist throughout many levels. A book on [[Level 4]] contains the earliest known record of '''level keys'''. It is a record, presumably from the 1800s, written by an unknown wanderer. The wanderer claims to have found a '''level key''' leading to Level 4 in [[Level 1]]. This claim cannot be verified, as it is impossible to track the movements of '''level keys'''. ==Example Level Keys== Not all '''level keys''' have been listed here, only a brief selection of those in the possession of the [[M.E.G.]] {| !Level !Appearance !Sound !Effects of [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]] |- |[[Level 0]] |Chevron depicted on level's wallpaper. |Hum-buzz of fluorescent lights, growing louder closer to an entrance to the level. |Chevron turned black. Hum-buzz ceased. |- |[[Level 3]] |Rusty pipe, with three pipe tightener rings serving as the key's teeth. |Sounds of heavy machinery and electricity crackling. |Sounds ceased. |- |[[Level 37]] |White tile, carved into the shape of an ornate key from the 18th century. |Gentle splashing of water. |White tile turned black. |- |[[Level 188]] |A wrought-iron key with a window in the head of the key. |None. |Space behind the window turned black. |- |[[Level 610]] |A package of snacks with a pointed end. |Crinkling of a plastic wrapper. |No change. |} {{Footnotes}}
